title: SmartPlaylist Definition
description: Defines how a playlist organizes, filters, sorts, and displays episodes for a podcast. Each definition file lives at {patternId}/playlists/{id}.json. Multiple definitions under the same config share the podcast's episode pool and claim episodes based on priority.

id.description: A unique identifier for this playlist within the config (e.g., 'season-1'). Must match the filename (without .json extension). Referenced by the pattern meta's playlists array to control display order.

displayName.description: The name shown to users in the app. For seasonNumber, year, and titleDiscovery resolvers, group names may be generated at runtime by the titleExtractor. For titleClassifier, group names come from the display names you define in groups.

priority.description: Controls the order playlists claim episodes. Lower numbers go first. If two playlists could match the same episode, the one with lower priority claims it. Playlists with filters always run before filter-less ones. Within each tier, lower priority numbers are processed first. Auto-assigned from playlist array order by the editor.

selector.description: Controls how resolver groups map to selector dropdown entries. When absent, all groups appear as cards inside a single entry. When partitionBy is 'group', each group becomes its own entry. When partitionBy is 'seasonNumber' or 'year', groups are organized under partition entries.

grouping.description: Defines how episodes are organized into groups. Contains the grouping method, discovery hints, numbering extraction, and static classifiers.

groupListing.description: How the group list is arranged. Controls sorting, user sort toggling, and section headers.

groupItem.description: Defaults for individual group card display. Overridable per-classifier in grouping.staticClassifiers[].groupItem.

episodeListing.description: How episodes are arranged within groups. Controls sorting and year headers. Overridable per-classifier.

episodeItem.description: Defaults for individual episode row display. Overridable per-classifier.

episodeFilters.description: Rules applied before organizing episodes. Having any filter makes this definition run before filter-less ones. Within each rule, all fields must match (AND). Across rules: require includes only when ALL rules match (AND); exclude rejects if ANY rule matches (OR).

require.description: Include rules (AND). An episode is included only when it matches ALL rules. Within each rule, all fields must match (AND).

exclude.description: Exclude rules (OR). An episode is excluded if it matches ANY rule. Within each rule, all fields must match (AND).

EpisodeFilterEntry.description: A single filter rule matched against episode fields. When multiple fields are specified, all must match (AND). At least one field must be specified.

title.description: A case-insensitive text pattern matched against the episode title. Use regular expression syntax for advanced matching.

description.description: A case-insensitive text pattern matched against the episode description. Use regular expression syntax for advanced matching.

YearBinding.description: Controls how groups relate to year sections. 'None' shows a flat list, 'pinToYear' places each group under its year, 'splitByYear' repeats a group for every year it spans.

oneOf.description: No year sections. Groups are displayed in a flat list.
Show group under its year. Each group appears once, placed under the year of its earliest episode.
Show group in each year it spans. A group appears multiple times, once for each year it has episodes in.

GroupDef.description: A group definition. Groups with a pattern match episodes by title; a group without a pattern serves as a catch-all for unmatched episodes. Groups are evaluated in order (first match wins). At most one catch-all should exist and should be placed last. Groups can override `groupListing`, `groupItem`, `episodeListing`, `episodeItem`, and `numberingExtractor` settings. Omitted fields use the playlist defaults.

id.description: A unique identifier for this group within the playlist.

displayName.description: The name shown to users for this group in the app. For titleClassifier, this is always used as-is (not overridden by titleExtractor).

pattern.description: A text pattern matched against episode titles. Episodes matching this pattern are assigned to this group. Omit this field to create a catch-all group for unmatched episodes.

groupListing.description: Per-group overrides for the group list (applied when this group appears in the list). Omitted fields use the playlist defaults.

yearBinding.description: Custom year-group relationship for this group. When not set, uses the playlist default.

groupItem.description: Per-group overrides for this group's card. Omitted fields use the playlist defaults.

showDateRange.description: Show or hide date range on this group's card. When not set, uses the playlist default.

episodeListing.description: Per-group overrides for the episode list inside this group. Omitted fields use the playlist defaults.

showYearHeaders.description: Show or hide year dividers in this group's episode list. When not set, uses the playlist default.

sort.description: Custom episode sort for this group. When not set, uses the playlist default.

episodeItem.description: Per-group overrides for individual episode rows inside this group. Omitted fields use the playlist defaults.

titleExtractor.description: Custom episode title transformation for this group. When not set, uses the playlist default.

numberingExtractor.description: Custom number rule for this group. When not set, uses the playlist default.

SortOrder.description: Ascending (A-Z, oldest first, lowest number first) or descending (Z-A, newest first, highest number first).

SortRule.description: What to sort groups by and in which direction.

oneOf.description: Sort by the group's number. For seasonNumber this is the season, for year the publication year, for titleDiscovery the first-appearance index, for titleClassifier the group's position.
Sort by the newest episode date in each group.

EpisodeSortRule.description: What to sort episodes by and in which direction.

oneOf.description: Sort by the episode's published date.
Sort by episode number (from feed data or numbering rule).

TitleExtractor.description: Generates a display name from episode data. Reads a value, optionally matches a pattern, and formats the result. Steps: (1) read the source field; (2) if pattern is set, find a match and extract the specified part; (3) if template is set, format with {value} placeholder; (4) if any step fails, try the fallback. Supports chaining multiple fallback steps for complex podcast formats.

source.description: Which part of the episode to read: 'title' (episode title), 'description' (episode description), 'seasonNumber' (season number from feed or numbering rule), or 'episodeNumber' (episode number from feed or numbering rule).

pattern.description: A text pattern to find in the source value. Use parentheses () to mark the part you want to extract. When omitted, the raw source value is used directly (useful with a template like 'Season {value}').

group.description: Which matched part to use (0 = entire match, 1 = first part in parentheses, etc.). Only meaningful when pattern is set.

template.description: How to format the extracted text. Use {value} as a placeholder. For example, 'Season {value}' turns '3' into 'Season 3'.

fallback.description: A fallback step tried when this one fails to find a match. Can be chained for multi-step fallbacks. Not tried when fallbackValue already returned a result.

fallbackValue.description: A default name used when the source is 'seasonNumber' or 'episodeNumber' and the value is missing or zero. Checked before pattern matching. Has no effect for 'title' or 'description' sources. Useful for labeling specials (e.g., 'Extras' for season 0).

NumberingExtractor.description: Finds season and episode numbers in episode text, overriding feed data when needed. Uses a three-tier approach: (1) primary pattern with parts for season and episode; (2) fallback pattern for special episodes with a fixed season; (3) feed data as last resort. Essential for podcasts that encode numbering in titles like '[62-15] Title'.

source.description: Which part of the episode to read: 'title' (most common) or 'description'.

pattern.description: A text pattern with parentheses () marking where the season and episode numbers appear. Example: '[(\d+)-(\d+)]' finds season from part 1 and episode from part 2.

seasonGroup.description: Which matched part contains the season number. Default is 1. Set to null to skip season detection (episode-only mode).

episodeGroup.description: Which matched part contains the episode number (default: 2).

fallbackSeasonNumber.description: Season number to assign when only the fallback pattern matches (e.g., 0 for special episodes).

fallbackEpisodePattern.description: A backup pattern tried when the primary one doesn't match. Useful for catching special or bonus episodes with a different title format.

fallbackEpisodeCaptureGroup.description: Which matched part in the fallback pattern contains the episode number (default: 1).

fallbackToRss.description: When no pattern matches, use the episode number from the feed data instead. This is the last resort. Only the episode number is used; season number is not set.

SelectorConfig.description: Controls how resolver groups map to selector dropdown entries in the app UI.

partitionBy.description: How to partition groups into selector entries.

titleExtractor.description: Generates display names for partitioned selector entries. Used when partitionBy is 'seasonNumber' or 'year'. Not needed for 'group' (group names are used directly).

GroupingConfig.description: Defines how episodes are organized into groups. The 'by' field selects the grouping strategy.

by.description: Determines how episodes are organized into groups.

oneOf.description: Groups episodes by their season number from feed data or titles.
Groups episodes by the year they were published.
Finds recurring patterns in episode titles and groups by them.
Groups episodes by matching titles against patterns defined in staticClassifiers.

discoveryHint.description: Regex pattern for titleDiscovery fallback. Replaces the v4 groups[0].pattern overload.

numberingExtractor.description: Finds season and episode numbers in episode text, overriding feed data when needed.

staticClassifiers.description: Group definitions for titleClassifier. Each entry defines a pattern for matching episode titles. Required when 'by' is 'titleClassifier'.

GroupListingConfig.description: Controls how the group list is arranged, sorted, and sectioned.

yearBinding.description: Controls how groups relate to year sections.

sort.description: Sort rule for ordering groups. When not set, groups appear in their natural order.

userSortable.description: When enabled, users can flip between ascending and descending order in the app.

GroupItemConfig.description: Default display settings for individual group cards. Overridable per-classifier in grouping.staticClassifiers[].groupItem.

showDateRange.description: When enabled, each group card shows the date range of its episodes.

pinToYear.description: When true, pin group to its earliest year's section.

prependSeasonNumber.description: When enabled, group titles are prefixed with the season number (e.g., 'S13 Lincoln Arc').

titleExtractor.description: Generates display names for group titles from episode data.

EpisodeListingConfig.description: Controls how episodes are arranged within groups. Overridable per-classifier.

sort.description: Sort order for episodes within groups. When not set, episodes are ordered by published date (oldest first).

showYearHeaders.description: When enabled, episode lists show year dividers.

EpisodeItemConfig.description: Default display settings for individual episode rows. Overridable per-classifier.

titleExtractor.description: Transforms episode display names. Strips redundant information conveyed by the group context.
